symptoms: Cold hands and feet turn blue: 3/10

meetings.summary: Review of ivermectin trial, T3 withdrawal effects, and Ketotifen (MCAS) strategy. Planning for upcoming 120h dry fast and T3 Scorch Protocol.
nextSteps: Start Ketotifen trial (0.25mg) to monitor sedative sensitivity, ramp to 0.5mg/1mg if tolerated
Read the entire Scorch Protocol (Phase 1-4) during the upcoming fast for deep mechanistic understanding
Print the T3 ramp-up schedule (printable page in Scorch guide)
Daily journal of symptoms/temperature for data-driven customization
meetings.notes:
Ivermectin: 5 days in. Day 1 showed temporary (2h) 90% brain fog reduction, but effect has not returned. Reacting well (no allergies).

Medications: Stopped T3 recently; noticed worsened cold hands/feet. Waking up groggy; needs YouTube stimulation to get out of bed. Ketotifen introduced for suspected MCAS (1mg dose available).

Scorch Protocol: Deep dive into SRT-3 Scorch Protocol. Discussed high doses (up to 240mcg) and managing supply during travel. Advised to print the ramp-up schedule.

Dry Fasting: 5-day scheduled for Jan 7. Discussed 'the wall' at Day 3 and expected brain clarity on Day 4/5 via deep ketosis.

Travel: Flying to US/India on Jan 18. Advised to continue T3 ramp-up on the plane. History of 10-year deterioration since 2016 (Waterloo math/science background).

meetings.summary: Check-in on Day 2 of 72h dry fast, review of symptoms and T3 usage, planning for next 5-day fast.
nextSteps: Perform light, leisurely walks (no sweating) and massage kidneys/liver if pain occurs
Refeed for ~6 days (Keppner diet) starting after current fast
Get blood tests next Friday [CBC, Liver, Kidney, Thyroid]
meetings.notes:
Fast Status: Day 2 of 72h. Symptoms included fatigue ('nap' led to clarity) and coffee withdrawal headaches. Transient RUQ pain (liver/kidney) noted.

Medications: Taking T3 (20mcg/day). Advised to split dose in future (AM/PM) but 1x/day ok for now. High HR (104->112) noted after breathwork. Avoid supplements during dry fast (except T3).

Action Plan: 10-day break between fasts. Start next 5-day fast Jan 7. Coach to prepare Calendar #2.

meetings.summary: Initial discussion on severe brain fog, histamine intolerance hypothesis, and planning Christmas dry fast.
nextSteps: Ask doctor for antihistamines (Ketotifen or Cyproheptadine) or get OTC Benadryl
meetings.notes:
Symptoms: Debilitating brain fog, esp. after eating (chicken). Suspected histamine intolerance. Low heart rate and temp.

Medications: Started T3 Dec 15 (5mcg), currently on 25mcg test dose. Antibiotics/Antihistamines discussed.

Diet: Low calorie (~1800), vegetarian lunch + pizza cravings. Goal to raise BMR to 3000 cal.

History: 'Long Covid' symptoms detailed.

meetings.summary: Post-36h dry fast check-in. Discussed fainting episode, high A1C (6.7), and plan to delay next fast to ramp up T3.
nextSteps: Delay 3-day Dry Fast to ~Dec 29 (allow 2 weeks T3 ramp-up)
Order 'Blood Supplement' (Ancestral Supplements) for iron/immune support
meetings.notes:
Fast Status: Completed 36h dry fast. Felt 'fat adapted'.

Adverse Event: Fainted day prior (dehydration + Aspirin + potential low iron?).

Lab Work: High A1C (6.7) noted from August, likely due to metabolic stress/starvation dieting vs sugar intake.

Plan: Slow down. Prioritize T3 ramp-up and Insulin Resistance (IR) strategy (Aspirin -> Inositol -> ALA).
